description_long: This object is a linear metal guard rail commonly used in urban environments to control pedestrian or vehicular movement. It features a horizontal top rail supported by evenly spaced vertical bars, all constructed from tubular metal, likely galvanized steel for durability and corrosion resistance. The base consists of two curved feet that provide stability and allow the rail to stand freely on flat surfaces. The ends of the rail are rounded, with no sharp protrusions, enhancing safety and minimizing snagging hazards. The overall design is utilitarian, with a smooth, metallic finish and no visible decorative elements or reflective markings. This type of guard rail is typically found along sidewalks, in parks, or at event perimeters to create temporary or semi-permanent barriers.
description: A metallic, tubular guard rail with vertical bars and rounded ends, supported by curved feet for freestanding stability. The surface is smooth and utilitarian, suitable for urban crowd or traffic control.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the thin profile and the curved base feet of the guard rail.
description_view_1: A frontal view displaying the horizontal top rail, evenly spaced vertical bars, and rounded ends.
description_view_2: Another narrow side view, identical to view 0, highlighting the slim profile and base curvature.
description_view_3: A rear view, visually similar to the front, showing the vertical bars and rounded rail ends.
